title: The Posthumous Papers of the Pickwick Club, v. 1 (of 2)
summaries: "The Posthumous Papers of the Pickwick Club, v. 1 (of 2)" by Charles Dickens is a novel serialized from March 1836 to November 1837. Samuel Pickwick and three companions journey through the English countryside, recording their adventures for their London club. Their travels bring comic misadventures, colorful characters, and unexpected troubles—including a romantic misunderstanding with Pickwick's landlady that leads to one of literature's most famous legal cases, landing both parties in debtor's prison. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
